title: Is the EU ready to drop unanimous voting?
content: The decisive defeat of Hungarian leader and EU veto-er-in-chief Viktor Orbán presents the EU with an opportunity to move forward on several key foreign policy issues. But was he the problem? Or is unanimity holding the EU back from defending itself on the world stage?

title: Zelenskyy says Patriot missile shortage 'could not be any worse'
content: Ukraine’s shortage of Patriot missiles could not be worse than it already is, Volodymyr Zelenskyy said, as the US Vice President, JD Vance, called ending aid to Ukraine "one of the proudest" achievements of the Trump administration.

title: Fact check: Does a video show Israeli soldiers pushing bodies from a rooftop?
content: Footage appearing to show Israeli soldiers handling bodies on a rooftop in the West Bank has gone viral, amassing millions of views. However, the video is old and dates back to September 2024.
snippet: Footage appearing to show Israeli soldiers handling bodies on a rooftop in the West Bank has gone viral, amassing millions of views. However, the video is old and dates back to September 2024.

title: Viktor Orbán to skip his last EU summit after bruising electoral defeat
content: The tradition in Brussels states that departing leaders are paid homage by their peers and given a goodbye token when they attend their last summit. Not for Orbán as he is now expected to skip the gathering.

title: Trump and Meloni: From close relations to a transatlantic crisis
content: The relationship between Trump and Meloni was typified by warm handshakes and even warmer words until the US leader did something considered by most Italians to be unthinkable: he criticised the pope.

title: Magyar keeps door open to Russian energy despite EU phase-out plans
content: At his first press conference after being elected Prime Minister of Hungary, Péter Magyar said on Monday that the country will continue seeking the cheapest energy sources, including from Russia. His statement appear to clash with earlier pledges to phase out Russian energy imports by 2035.

title: Israel needs to stop encouraging settler violence, Palestinian FM says

title: No, the EU hasn't set up a UK harmonisation office in Brussels to help undo Brexit

title: Péter Magyar accuses outgoing foreign minister of destroying confidential documents
content: Hungarian Prime Minister-elect Peter Magyar has accused outgoing Foreign Minister Péter Szijjártó of shredding confidential sanctions-related documents at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, citing insider sources during a press conference in Budapest on Monday. The ministry has not yet responded.

title: How a Russian propaganda unit is targeting Hungary’s elections

title: EU considering excessive profit taxes on oil and gas companies, foreign profits remain unclear

title: European Parliament proposes 10% increase in EU long-term budget

title: Energy prices set to rise as last Gulf Tankers reach Europe, analysts say
